macro_segments.title: The Orlah Counting System: Planting Methods and the Thirty-Day Rule

micro_segments.title: Introduction to Rosh Hashanah for Planting (Netia) vs. Other Agricultural Cycles
Three Methods of Planting: Direct Planting, Bending (Mavrikh), and Grafting (Markiv)
The Thirty-Day Rule Before Shmita and Its Application to Orlah
Fruit Restrictions and the Transition from Netia to Ilan: Orlah and Reva'i Years

macro_segments.title: The Complex Calendar: When Orlah and Reva'i Expire Relative to Rosh Hashanah and Tu B'Shvat

micro_segments.title: Year-Counting and the Role of Tu B'Shvat for Established Trees
The Misalignment Problem: Why the Third Year Extends Beyond Rosh Hashanah Until Tu B'Shvat
Scriptural Basis from Leviticus: The Verses About Orlah and Reva'i Years

macro_segments.title: The Dispute Over the Thirty-Day Requirement: Rabbi Meir vs. Rabbi Eliezer and the Role of Rooting Time

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Meir's Position: One Day Counts as a Year (Based on Cow Analogy)
Rava's Kal Vachomer: Why the Beginning of a Year Should Count Like the End (Niddah Parallel)
The Niddah Example: Why Partial Days Count at the Beginning but Not at the End
Trees vs. Cows: Different Calendrical Systems (Calendar Dates vs. Birthdays)
Rabbi Eliezer's Requirement: The Role of Rooting Time (30, 3, or 14 Days) in Orlah Counting

macro_segments.title: Reconciling the Thirty-Day Rule with Rooting Time: The Gemara's Resolution Using Rabbi Meir

micro_segments.title: The Problem: Why Thirty Days Alone Doesn't Satisfy Orlah Requirements If Rooting Takes Additional Time
The Solution: The Last Day Performs Double Duty—Rooting Occurs Within the 30th Day
Conclusion for Shmita: The Thirty-Day Rule Addresses Rooting Time, Not the Tosefet Period

macro_segments.title: The Prooftext Debate: Interpreting Genesis on the 601st Year to Determine If One Day or One Month Counts

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Meir's Interpretation: One Day in a Year Counts as a Full Year (Vayehi B'Achat)
Rabbi Eliezer's Interpretation: One Day in a Month Means One Month Counts as a Year
The Analogy Derived: Day to Month as Month to Year (Therefore 30 Days = One Year)

macro_segments.title: Historical and Eschatological Timeline: Rabbi Eliezer's Tishrei-Centered Year vs. Rabbi Yehoshua's Nissan-Centered Year

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer: Creation, Patriarchs, and Future Redemption in Tishrei; Pesach Only for Isaac's Birth
The Shared Agreement on Remembrance: Sarah, Rachel, Hannah, and Joseph Released in Tishrei (Rosh Hashanah)
Rabbi Yehoshua: Nissan as Primary Month for Creation and Patriarchs, with Tishrei Retained for Divine Remembrance
